results.title: Fieldy Hands On Review: Can this AI Wearable help my ADHD brain remember tasks and save my marriage? : Fieldy Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Holden Lewis

This is a really thorough review, thanks Gabe! I tried a wearable AI called Bee and was forced to quit using it after three days. Ultimately, none of the people in my life were comfortable with AI snooping on our interactions and it became too cumbersome to turn off and on all the time (especially for an ADHD-head like myself).   
  
Did you feel the need to disclose you were wearing it? How did people in your life feel?

Report

Gabe Perez

Product Hunt

Product Hunt

@cholden\_lewis I feel that! I actually didn't like the form factor of Bee. I much rather a pendant or a necklace. I don't always feel the need to disclose that I'm wearing it unless it's a really private or sensitive conversation.

Report

Holden Lewis [...] Report

Martynas Krupskis

Fieldy

Fieldy

@nathansudds we used to be called Compass, you might have heard that!

Report

Gabe Perez

Product Hunt

Product Hunt

@nathansudds I honestly cannot believe Omi slipped my mind, I was a big fan of the form factor! I need to get my hands one on to try it out. I’ll update my review this week - Omi should def be in there.

Report

Gabe Perez

Product Hunt

Product Hunt

@nathansudds Omi is now featured as well!

Report

Dan Bulteel

Meet-Ting

Meet-Ting

Great review, talks to my brain on a level too 🫣😁

Report

Subscribe [...] My biggest gripe though is multilingual support is notably absent—a major drawback if, like me, your day is a blend of English, Japanese, and Spanish. I had many conversations in Japanese that I would have loved to look back on but instead the transcription just noted the conversation was in Japanese. So it looks like the ability is there and may come in a further update.

Real-World Use: The Pawsome, the Meh-ow, and the Spurrprising

The Pawsome: I can say confidently that Fieldy genuinely came in handy. It reminded me of many things my wife asked me to do… that… I had forgotten. I got into the habit of checking my new tasks during breaks and it actually helped reshape my whole day updating work tasks based on meetings or daddy duty pick up times.

I like the data, but its been shutting off mid read lately, yet still 'reading' my heartbeat when my finger is off the pad, to the point of changing the numbers slightly here n there like I'm still attached. Seems very fishy to me. Almost bought lifetime till this happened

Really insightfully designed. Lots of valuable metrics that can lead to better, healthier choices and habits. It's only been one day but I'm really enjoying this app. Can wait to collect a but more info and see some interesting trends.

Report

 24 views [...] If you want to check whether you’re stressed right now, take a look at the Daily Timeline chart. If you’d like to take a measurement, you can do that in the app. Just tap the red button in the top menu — that’s the way to measure using your camera.

Report

•2 reviews

A pretty good app. The info it provides seems pretty good for a phone app. It's obviously not as good as proper medical equipment at a hospital. I don't think any app can do that. But I do find the info it gives me helpful/useful. The tips and hints, + the in-app explanations & educational material are pretty good too. It is well designed, and reasonably easy to get the hang of. The money I paid for this app feels well spent. Thanks Welltory. Keep up the great work.

results.title: Formaloo Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What's great

Ritik Singh Panwar

#### What's great

Sheida Mirjahani
X Space Repurposer

X Space Repurposer

Farokh Shahabi
Formaloo

Formaloo

Peter Bachmann Dahlstrøm

#### What's great [...] #### vs Alternatives

I had looked at Typeform, Google Forms, and JotForm before settling on Formaloo. Typeform is great for UX but the pricing becomes steep quickly for multiple forms. Google Forms is too basic for professional client work. JotForm has a lot of features but can feel overwhelming. Formaloo struck the best balance, powerful enough for complex workflows, easy enough to use, and the free tier meant I could test it thoroughly before committing. The ability to handle unlimited responses on the free plan was a huge selling point for my client projects.

Amirali Pirouzbakhsh

#### What's great

It collapses multiple tools into one.

It respects non-technical users without being dumbed down.

The output looks professional by default

Real analytics, not just data collection. [...] Real analytics, not just data collection.

It's free to start.

#### What needs improvement

Mostly small UI polish stuff. There were some quirks in the past that the team has already fixed, but a few minor ones still pop up here and there, nothing that gets in the way of actually using the product, just little details that could use refinement. Honestly, the team ships fast and clearly listens to feedback, so I'd expect these to get sorted over time.

#### vs Alternatives

Google Forms

It's not just forms. Google Forms is a form tool, full stop. Formaloo is a no-code platform, you can build dashboards, client portals, CRMs, surveys with conditional logic, calculation forms, even chatbots. Once you outgrow basic forms (and most people do), Google Forms hits a wall fast.

Dinno León
